game_name: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles II: The Arcade Game

sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system documentation available in sources

special_mechanics.name: Jump Kick
special_mechanics.description: In Japanese version, jump kick can be performed at any point during jump. In western versions, only while descending. Character performs aerial kick attack.

special_mechanics.name: Special Attack
special_mechanics.description: Performed by holding attack button and pressing jump. Raphael rolls along ground and finishes with kick; other turtles perform sweeping jump attack with weapons.

special_mechanics.name: Wall Spring/Wall Jump
special_mechanics.description: Turtles can spring off walls in certain areas of levels.

special_mechanics.name: Direction Change Post-Attack
special_mechanics.description: In Japanese version only, for a few frames after jump kick or special attack, player can push opposite direction to switch which way attack is aimed.

special_mechanics.name: Throw Mechanic
special_mechanics.description: Turtles can throw Foot soldiers overhead as special move.

tier_notes: Tier 2 empirical/qualitative data. Sources document gameplay mechanics but contain no precise physics parameters (velocities, accelerations, gravity values in pixels/frame, sub-pixel formats). Regional differences between Japanese and western versions affect mechanics (jump kick timing, attack direction switching, enemy health values). Game is arcade port with simplified NES adaptation.
sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor (gameplay mechanics, regional differences, character-specific attack patterns)
wikipedia - Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles arcade game article (gameplay description, special moves, wall mechanics)
